topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the blank screen of his laptop, the cursor blinking mockingly in the center. The afternoon sun slanted across his desk, illuminating dust motes dancing in the stagnant air. He tapped his pen against his textbook cover, the rhythmic sound the only thing breaking the quiet hum of the empty classroom. He scrolled through his feed, a mindless drift through curated perfection that offered no stimulation. Then, a notification popped up. It was a direct message from Ms. Albright. Liam blinked, a slow, heavy motion. He clicked it, expecting a standard query about an assignment. Instead, there was a picture of a perfectly arranged bookshelf, captioned with a cryptic, one-word reflection. A strange, dull thrumming started in his chest. He reread the message three times, the lack of immediate urgency making him pause, his fingers hovering over the reply button.

[story 2]

Maya was tracing the pattern of the worn carpet with the toe of her sneaker, her gaze fixed on the distant ceiling tiles. The lecture had concluded an hour ago, and the silence in the hallway felt thick, suffocating. She picked up her phone, intending to watch a short, frivolous video, but her thumb froze over the social media app. A profile picture flashed on the screen—Ms. Albright, smiling brightly in front of a vibrant cityscape. A quick glance at the recent posts revealed photos of garden tours and obscure historical sites. Maya felt a distinct lack of engagement; the usual spark that usually animated her seemed entirely absent. Curiosity nudged her forward. She navigated to the direct messages. There, was a recent comment she had made on a class forum, subtly edited, and now, a reply from Ms. Albright, acknowledging her input with an almost unnerving familiarity. A slow, almost imperceptible smile touched the corners of her mouth.

[story 3]

For weeks, Alex had been meticulously rearranging the collection of loose change in his pocket, the small metallic clinking providing a monotonous soundtrack to his inertia. He felt adrift, the routine of school days stretching out like an endless, beige corridor. He was zoning out during the mandatory study hall, letting his attention wander aimlessly until his phone buzzed insistently. It was a notification from his history teacher. Alex frowned, annoyed by the intrusion. He opened the app, and there, was a photo—a candid shot of the teacher laughing during a faculty event, clearly taken moments before. Beneath it, a short, personal note confirming she was actively monitoring his activity. Alex let the phone slip from his grasp, the screen momentarily illuminating his slack expression. A strange, restless energy began to vibrate beneath his skin. He picked the phone up again, his movements suddenly imbued with a new, uncharacteristic tension.
